author: @maxkennedy5073
text: Showing our age but Pertwee (3rd Dr) and Bessie were a classic.  That was a long time ago, was 7 or 8.  Where does the time go more than 1/2 a century later.  Looking forward to moving through the video's and seeing what you come up with oh master scavenger.

text: You are absolutely awesome! Thank you for sharing your knowledge!

author: @RAMSHACKLE28
text: I love your projects, thank you, cant wait to see more on this one.

author: @andrewradford3953
text:
Just converted an old Toro I was gifted, to run a rudimentary electric drive, with a $100au Milwaukee drill.
I started with a 30mm diameter pulley on the drill and I may have created one if the slowest land based vehicles.
Ilk add this to my YouTube zero turn conversion video when we get some time to film on the farm.
Can't wait to see what Robert does for this conversion. Maybe a 14S 48v Nissan Leaf pack?

author: @NorthernKitty
text: How you can juggle so many projects at once is beyond me!!  I have a hard enough time focusing on just one job at a time, LOL!!!  😋  I've been working on repairing my snowblower for 3 days now, and still can't seem to finish it.  Crossing my fingers tomorrow is the day, as the snow flies again this weekend.  If not, I suppose I'll just hibernate in my workshop the rest of the winter.  😄   (Edit &  Update:  Finally fixed it today!!  With just a little bit of improvisation... I think I just created a new genre, "Snowpunk". 😁)

author: @teltwosheds2972
text: Rear axle is live so no need for bearings. Front wheels on some models have bearings cheap ones have bushes. What you are doing, has been done before with varying degrees of success. Can't wait to see how you go about it 😀.

author: @xspotbox4400

At least this is the way i imagine micro electric car, it will be interesting to see alternative approach in the coming episodes.

author: @BensWorkshop
text: If you want to make it look Edwardian you'll want a tiller instead of a steering wheel and a bonnet like a veteran Renault or similar. Nice idea. For other ideas you could look at the Victorian electric (battery) taxis they have in the Science museum.

replies.author: @kennickel878
replies.text: Stretch it, add angled solar panels as a top, and mount your gasifier, generator, and batteries to it to make it the parade float equivalent of a mobile power plant. Mount the bulk of the battery bank on the ground or in a trailer so that you can disconnect from the house Power Supply and still go run saws on the back 40. Or haul the whole thing off in event of evacuation order and power the emergency shelter despite downed lines.

author: @rastamanralph6670
text:
Why electric! I think people should be building things with engines in or pretty soon there won't be any left.
I'm not a fan of electric cars because it's a con. So much energy is used with engines to mine and build the car and batteries and people think they are more friendly to the environment. Plus there's not enough power comes from wind farms. If the whole country was covered in wind mills there still wouldn't be enough power to to run everything in the country! Did you know that when there's no wind the blades have to keep moving and they use a big diesel engine.

author: @leefoster4133
text: Are you going to be able to reuse the gearbox?  Wished I was back in the UK.  I have some ideas on for it.  My problem is the job sucks up most of my time and I can't be inventive.

author: @tinkmarshino
text: To short Rob.. We have all torn stuff down and cleaned it.. And you are right a 101 different ideas pound through my head.. Excitement is the game and quick is the action.. But I guess you are just building excitement for this one.. it is working.. I am hooked already.. you should get Luke involved in some of these projects.. Though I am sure you are used to working alone like me and it is hard to incorporate some one else unless they think just like you and are on the same page.. which is hard to find at any time.. Well carry on!
